name: The Guardian Content API Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary for The Guardian Content API covering news content, editorial taxonomy, and media types.

terms.definition: A piece of Guardian content such as an article, liveblog, gallery, video, or interactive.
The unique path-based identifier for a content item (e.g., technology/2024/jan/01/article-slug).
A top-level editorial category of The Guardian (e.g., technology, world, business, sport).
A keyword, contributor, series, or tone label used to categorize Guardian content.
A regional edition of The Guardian: UK, US, Australia, International, or Europe.
The ISO 8601 timestamp when content was published on the Guardian website.
The headline or display title of content as shown on the Guardian website.
The public URL for a content item or taxonomy term on theguardian.com.
The URL for retrieving a content item or taxonomy term via the Content API.
The access tier of the API key: developer (test), approved, or internal.
Optional additional metadata fields for content items (headline, byline, body, thumbnail, etc.).
Query parameter to request specific additional fields in content responses.
Query parameter to include tag data in content responses.
Sort order for search results: newest, oldest, or relevance.
Number of results per page in paginated responses (max 50 for developer tier).
A continuously updated content type for breaking news coverage.
A photo gallery content type.
An interactive data journalism content type.
A crossword puzzle content type.
The author attribution for a piece of Guardian content.
A short introductory paragraph summarizing an article, displayed beneath the headline.
A short promotional text used for article previews and social sharing.
